title: Rule-Aware Peer-Context Experts
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop work highlights clean air, undercut/overcut, and nearby drivers’ pit actions as important.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop work highlights clean air, undercut/overcut, and nearby drivers’ pit actions as important. FIA rules also create a distinct dry-race mandatory-stop regime. Current best code has little causal peer context.
implementation_hint: Add within-race causal peer features such as `driver_ahead_pitted_now`, `driver_behind_pitted_now`, recent pit-wave count on the same lap, field-relative lap-time percentile, pack density by position band, and a `mandatory_dry_stop_debt` flag derived from compound history under FIA dry-race rules. Train separate experts for dry first-stop, dry later-stop, and wet/intermediate regimes, then blend by regime.
expected_effect: Captures strategic contagion and rule-state effects that self-only tyre heuristics miss, especially around first-stop windows.
risk: No explicit gaps/flags are available, so peer-context proxies may be noisy and require strict causal construction.
